integration_validation_v2.gap: MT-192 requires Flight Recorder events for visual capture/validation/recovery. No such FR events exist. Cited mt_192_* tests prove state-replay determinism/gap-detection (WP-KERNEL-004), emitting FR-EVT-REPLAY-* events — not visual/validation/recovery events.
integration_validation_v2.evidence: tests/session_checkpoint_tests.rs:1499-1506 (asserts FR-EVT-REPLAY-STARTED/PROGRESS/COMPLETED — replay, not visual/validation/recovery); src/atelier/state_probe.rs:1044 (VISUAL_EVIDENCE matrix is MT-175, not an MT-192 FR-event surface)

integration_validation_v3.evidence: Impl: workflow_event_kinds.rs:53-57,75-77 (VisualCapture/VisualValidation/VisualRecovery = FR-EVT-VISUAL-*, mt_owner()=MT-192, required fields capture_ref/verdict/recovery_action); shared write path + migration 0116:73-75 CHECK pairing. Proof: tests/atelier_dcc_flight_recorder_tests.rs:318 mt192 - persists capture/validation(fail)/recovery chained on one capture_ref, re-reads from managed PostgreSQL by kind+mt_owner, EventLedger evidence, rejects machine-local session_ref at persistence boundary.
integration_validation_v3.gap: Legacy WP-004 mt_192_* replay test names cosmetic.
integration_validation_v3.basis: v3 adversarial per-MT review of remediated deliverable + runtime proof via full-crate managed-PG sweep and kernel-builder fresh-DB proof logs

minimum_controls: Use contract-first identity and preserve projection hash.
risks: Imported MT contract may under-capture prose-only review or proof instructions.
